content:
# Numbers Chapter 10 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 159–160; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0119–0120 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_010_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_010_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_010_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_010_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## The Silver Trumpets (vv. 1–10)

**10:1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**10:2** "Make for yourself two trumpets of silver; of hammered work you shall make them. And they shall be for you for the summoning of the congregation and for the setting out of the camps.

**10:3** And when they blow on both of them, all the congregation shall assemble to you at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

**10:4** And if they blow on one, then the leaders, the heads of the thousands of Israel, shall assemble to you.

**10:5** And when you blow an alarm, the camps that are encamped on the east side shall set out.

**10:6** And when you blow an alarm a second time, the camps that are encamped on the south side shall set out. An alarm shall be blown for their setting out.

**10:7** But when assembling the assembly, you shall blow, but you shall not sound an alarm.

---

## The Priestly Statute (v. 8–10)

**10:8** And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow on the trumpets; and they shall be for you as a perpetual statute throughout your generations.

**10:9** And when you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, then you shall sound an alarm on the trumpets, and you shall be remembered before YHWH your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.

**10:10** And on the day of your gladness, and at your appointed feasts, and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow on the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; and they shall be for you as a memorial before your God. I am YHWH your God."

---

## The Departure from Sinai (vv. 11–13)

**10:11** And it came to pass in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud was taken up from over the Tabernacle of the Testimony.

**10:12** And the Sons of Israel set out on their journeys from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud settled in the wilderness of Paran.

**10:13** And they set out for the first time at the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

---

## The Order of March (vv. 14–28)

**10:14** And the standard of the camp of the sons of Judah set out first, according to their armies, and over its army was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.

**10:15** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Issachar was Nethanel the son of Zuar.

**10:16** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.

**10:17** And the Tabernacle was taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set out, bearing the Tabernacle.

**10:18** And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, according to their armies, and over its army was Elizur the son of Shedeur.

**10:19** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.

**10:20** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.

**10:21** And the Kohathites set out, bearing the holy things; and the Tabernacle was set up before their coming.

**10:22** And the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim set out, according to their armies, and over its army was Elishama the son of Ammihud.

**10:23** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.

**10:24** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.

**10:25** And the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan set out, the rearguard for all the camps, according to their armies, and over its army was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.

**10:26** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ochran.

**10:27** And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.

**10:28** These were the journeyings of the Sons of Israel according to their armies. And they set out.

---

## Moses and Hobab (vv. 29–32)

**10:29** And Moses said to Hobab the son of Reuel the Midianite, the father-in-law of Moses, "We are setting out for the place of which YHWH said, 'I will give it to you.' Come with us and we will do good to you, for YHWH has spoken good concerning Israel."

**10:30** And he said to him, "I will not go, but rather to my own land and to my own kindred I will go."

**10:31** And he said, "Please do not leave us, for you know our encamping in the wilderness, and you shall be for us as eyes.

**10:32** And it shall be, if you go with us, then whatever good YHWH does for us, we will do the same for you."

---

## The Ark Sets Out (vv. 33–36)

**10:33** And they set out from the mountain of YHWH a journey of three days, and the ark of the covenant of YHWH went before them a journey of three days, to seek out a resting place for them.

**10:34** And the cloud of YHWH was over them by day when they set out from the camp.

**10:35** And it came to pass, when the ark set out, that Moses said, "Arise, O YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee before you!"

**10:36** And when it rested, he said, "Return, O YHWH, to the ten thousands of the thousands of Israel."

---

## Council Notes

SOPHIA evaluated all thirty-six verses of Numbers 10 across the three translator renderings (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA). The Leningrad Codex (pp. 159–160) and Codex Vaticanus (pa_0119–0120) were consulted throughout. The MT and LXX are in close agreement for this chapter, with notable divergences including: (1) v. 6, where the LXX adds third and fourth alarm signals for the western and northern camps not found in the MT; (2) v. 34, where the LXX adds "overshadowing" (skiazousa) to the cloud's function; (3) v. 35, where the LXX adds "all" (pantes) before "who hate you"; (4) v. 36, where the LXX reverses "myriads of thousands" to "thousands of myriads." The consensus follows the MT in all cases.

**General principles applied:** (1) Preserve "And" at the start of verses per binding convention for conjunctions. (2) Maintain "Sons of Israel" throughout. (3) Use "YHWH" transliterated. (4) Numbers rendered in English words per binding convention. (5) Prefer formal register that preserves Hebrew structure while producing clear English. (6) "Tent of Meeting" for Ohel Mo'ed; "Tabernacle of the Testimony" for Mishkan ha-Edut. (7) Preserve "at the mouth of YHWH" for the anthropomorphic Hebrew idiom al pi YHWH.

**Manuscript observations:** The Leningrad Codex shows Numbers 10 beginning on page 159, with a closed parashah marker (samekh) between v. 10 and v. 11. The inverted nuns (nun menuzeret) bracketing vv. 35–36 are clearly visible on page 160. The Vaticanus text across pa_0119–0120 follows the MT closely in this chapter, with the expansion at v. 6 and minor additions noted above.

**Key decisions:** The marching-order section (vv. 14–28) presented the highest degree of agreement among the three translators, with differences limited to minor phrasing choices ("according to their armies" vs. "company by company"; "standard of the camp" vs. "division under the banner"). The Hobab dialogue (vv. 29–32) likewise showed strong convergence. The trumpet legislation (vv. 1–10) and the Ark utterances (vv. 35–36) occasioned the most significant disagreements, particularly around the rendering of teru'ah ("alarm" vs. "war-blast") and the poetic register of the Ark sayings.

Full verse-by-verse comparison of all disagreements is recorded in `disagreements/NUM_010.md`.
